summary: Rule chunk from The Rules page 90, chunk 5.

mechanics.raw_text:
Inercontinetal 
‘A vehicle Is mostly a way to get player characters from point A to point Ball you teal- ly want it to do is provide some comic relief in between, Comic relief in Paranoia usually means horrible danger. As a result, PCs fre- quently don’t get to point B. That's no pro bem; activate the next set of clones and start them over. They won't make the same mistake twice, and eventually someone will get to B Don't worry about making. your vehicles “too powerful,” that i, giving them too much inthe way of armor or weapons. You can give Vehicle as much power as you want — just don't let the PCs use it, Vulture Warrior 920 is an example; its controlling bot brain has definite ideas of its own and is not about to let a bunch of maggots tel it what do to. You can get the same effect in a number of other ‘ways. For example, since a vehicle's capabili- ties are classified higher than the PCs, they may not know how powerful itis, and could not Corder it to use, e@, its laser cannon. If they did, they would be displaying treasonous knowledge. Don't worry about plausibility; R&D is always coming up with cockeyed gadgets. You an always claim a vehicle was experimental if it proves too silly. Don't worry about “game balance;” there's always a way to crock a device so the players don't get much use out of it and The Computer is always around if things start to get out of hand),
